response: Yes, both Daniel Mann and Todd Solondz were directors. Daniel Mann was an American film and television director, known for his work in the 20th century. Todd Solondz was an American independent film director and screenwriter, recognized for his dark, socially conscious satirical films. Mann directed films such as "A Prairie Home Companion" and "Desperately Seeking Susan," while Solondz directed films like "Welcome to the Dollhouse," "Storytelling," and "Life During Wartime."
